# Clang frontend crashes on invalid usage of `[[deprecated]]` inside `constexpr` function

**Author:** Rush10233
**URL:** https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/144264
**Status:** Closed
**Labels:** clang:frontend, crash-on-invalid, confirmed, generated by fuzzer
**Closed Date:** 2025-06-16T15:17:48Z

## Body

Generated by fuzzer that randomly inserts `[[deprecated]]` specifier before function and struct declarations.

Probably starts from clang-6: https://godbolt.org/z/c34Y65Tjn.

Simplified test:

```c++
constexpr void Test6() 
{ 
    using TT = struct [[deprecated]] T[deprecated{}; 
}
```

Stack Dump:

```
PLEASE submit a bug report to https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/ and include the crash backtrace, preprocessed source, and associated run script.

1.	<eof> parser at end of file
```

## Comments

### Comment 1 - tbaederr

Since `ParseAliasDeclarationAfterDeclarator` can easily return null, e.g. here:
https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/blob/89f692a24f6a13ae5cf9e37f91abe6f34c403258/clang/lib/Parse/ParseDeclCXX.cpp#L841

I thin we should simply check that `AD` is non-null here:
https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/blob/89f692a24f6a13ae5cf9e37f91abe6f34c403258/clang/lib/Parse/ParseDeclCXX.cpp#L761-L762

---
